brand_origins.notes: 107 Beauty is a South Korean skincare brand founded in 2015, specializing in fermented skincare products. Their Soseo Vinbiome Vitamin C Serum is among their offerings.
1More is a privately held audio company headquartered in San Diego, California and Shenzhen, China, founded in 2013, with no Canadian headquarters or manufacturing presence.

AirDoctor, LLC is headquartered in Sherman Oaks, California (United States), and its products are designed in California but finally assembled in China; there is no indication of Canadian ownership, headquarters, or manufacturing.

Trademark filings list the applicant Guangzhou Fengruijing Technology Device Co., Ltd. at an address in Guangzhou, China; there is no indication the brand is headquartered in or manufactures products in Canada.
Airpura was founded in 2004 in Quebec, Canada, and all units are manufactured at their facilities in Laval.
Alastin Skincare is headquartered in Carlsbad, California, USA, and is not a Canadian company. Their products are not manufactured in Canada.
Alen Corporation is headquartered in Austin, Texas, USA, and there is no indication that its air purifiers are manufactured in Canada.
Allies of Skin is a skincare brand founded and headquartered in Singapore, specializing in vitamin C serums. Their products are not manufactured in Canada.
Allseating is a Canadian office seating company headquartered in Mississauga, Ontario.

AmazonBasics is Amazon's private label brand, launched in 2009, offering a wide range of products, including drip coffee makers. The brand is headquartered in Seattle, Washington, USA, and is not a Canadian company. There is no evidence to suggest that AmazonBasics products are made, assembled, or manufactured in Canada.

Anker Innovations Co., Ltd is a Chinese electronics manufacturer founded in Shenzhen and headquartered in Changsha, Hunan, China; there is no evidence of Canadian ownership or manufacturing in Canada.

Anker Soundcore is a Chinese audio product brand established in 2018 as a sub-brand of Anker Innovations, specializing in audio products such as noise-cancelling over-ear headphones. The brand is headquartered in Shenzhen, China, and is not a Canadian company. There is no evidence to suggest that Soundcore products are made or manufactured in Canada.
Apple Inc. is an American company headquartered in Cupertino, California, and its noise-cancelling over-ear headphones (AirPods Max) are manufactured outside Canada.

Audio-Technica is a Japanese company founded in 1962, headquartered in Machida, Tokyo, Japan. It designs and manufactures professional audio equipment, including turntables, but is not a Canadian company and does not manufacture its products in Canada.

Austin Air Systems is headquartered in Buffalo, NY, USA, and all assembly and manufacturing of its air purifiers occur there (Made in America), with products distributed internationally including Canada.
Avid is a British manufacturer specializing in high-end turntables and audio equipment, founded in 1995 and headquartered in Cambridge, England.
Basyx is a brand of office furniture owned by The HON Company, which is headquartered in Muscatine, Iowa, USA. There is no evidence to suggest that Basyx products are manufactured in Canada.

BeautyStat is a skincare company founded by Ron Robinson, a veteran cosmetic chemist, in New York, NY, USA. Their products, including the Universal C Skin Refiner vitamin C serum, are manufactured in the United States.
Belif is a South Korean skincare brand, and its products, including the Vitamin C serum, are manufactured in South Korea.
No verifiable information found regarding a company named 'Berlman' producing office chairs in Canada.
Blueair was founded in Stockholm, Sweden in 1996 and remains headquartered there; it is part of the Unilever family and there is no indication of Canadian ownership or manufacturing.
bObsweep Inc. is headquartered in Toronto and was founded in Canada; its products are designed in the USA and Canada but assembled globally.
Bonavita is a trademark of Eternal East (HK) Ltd, a Hong Kong corporation; its products are printed and manufactured in China, with no Canadian headquarters or manufacturing.
Bose is an American company founded in Massachusetts, headquartered in Framingham; its manufacturing facilities are in the US, Mexico, China, and Malaysia, with no Canadian production sites.
Bowers & Wilkins is a British company founded and headquartered in Worthing, West Sussex, England, with no evidence of Canadian ownership or manufacturing in Canada.

Braun GmbH is a German consumer products company founded in Frankfurt in 1921 and headquartered in Kronberg im Taunus, Germany; its drip coffee makers are manufactured overseas (primarily in China and formerly Romania), not in Canada.
Breville is an Australian brand founded in Sydney in 1932 and is not headquartered or owned in Canada; its appliances are manufactured primarily in Asia, not in Canada.
Brondell is headquartered in San Francisco, California, USA, and does not manufacture its products in Canada.

BTOD, also known as Beyond The Office Door, is an American company specializing in office furniture, including ergonomic chairs. Their headquarters are located in Weston, Wisconsin, United States, and they do not manufacture their products in Canada.
BUNN (Bunn-O-Matic Corporation) is an American-founded beverage equipment manufacturer headquartered in Illinois, with production plants in the United States.
Founded in 1996 and headquartered in Quebec City; the company designs and manufactures coffee equipment with production sites in Canada.
Calphalon is an American‐founded cookware and small appliances brand headquartered in Georgia under Newell Brands; its drip coffee makers and other products are not manufactured in Canada.

Cambridge Audio is a British manufacturer of audio equipment, including turntables, founded in 1968. Its headquarters are located in London, England, and it is owned by Audio Partnership Plc. The company does not have manufacturing facilities in Canada.
Hand-crafted pour-over coffee makers designed, manufactured, and headquartered in Toronto, Canada.

brand_origins.headquarters_location: New York, New York, United States
brand_origins.notes: CeraVe is a U.S.-based skincare brand owned by L'Oréal, and its products are not manufactured in Canada.
The Chairman is an Australian company specializing in ergonomic office chairs, with no known Canadian operations.
CleanAirKits is a U.S.-based family business headquartered and assembled in Missouri, with no Canadian ownership or manufacturing.

Colamy is a U.S.-based company founded in 2019, specializing in ergonomic office and home seating. Their products are sold in Canada, but there is no evidence indicating that they are manufactured or assembled in Canada.

The Corsi-Rosenthal Foundation is a U.S.-based non-profit organization dedicated to promoting clean indoor air and supplying air purifiers to those in need. The foundation was established by Dr. Richard Corsi and Jim Rosenthal, both based in the United States. Their DIY air purifier design, known as the Corsi-Rosenthal Box, has been widely adopted in various countries, including Canada, but the organization itself is not Canadian.

Cougar is a Taiwanese company specializing in gaming hardware and accessories, including office chairs. Their headquarters are located in Tainan, Taiwan, and there is no evidence to suggest that their products are manufactured in Canada.
Coway was founded and is headquartered in South Korea; its air purifiers are manufactured in Korea, not Canada.
Crosley is an American company headquartered in Louisville, Kentucky, and its products are not manufactured in Canada.
Cuckoo is a Malaysian company headquartered in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, and its products are manufactured in China.
Cuisinart is an American brand headquartered in Stamford, Connecticut; its products are manufactured outside Canada.

Dermalogica is an American company headquartered in Carson, California, and is not Canadian-owned or headquartered in Canada. Its products, including the Biolumin-C Vitamin C Serum, are not manufactured in Canada.

No evidence of a Canadian air purifier brand named DIY; the only identifiable ‘DIY’ company is an education/service firm headquartered in Bengaluru, India, and other uses of “DIY” in air purification refer to do-it-yourself kits or guides, not a Canadian-based manufacturer.

Dr. Dennis Gross Skincare is an American brand founded in 2000 by Dr. Dennis Gross and his wife, Carrie Gross. The company is headquartered in New York City, USA, and is not a Canadian company. There is no evidence to suggest that their products are made in Canada.
Dr. Loretta is a U.S.-based skincare brand founded by dermatologist Dr. Loretta Ciraldo, specializing in vitamin C serums and other skincare products.
Dreame Technology is a Chinese consumer electronics company founded in 2017 and headquartered in Suzhou, China; its products are manufactured in China, not Canada.

Drunk Elephant is a Houston-based skincare brand founded in 2012 by Tiffany Masterson. It was acquired by Shiseido in 2019. The brand's vitamin C serum, C-Firma Fresh Vitamin-C Day Serum, is available in Canada through retailers like Sephora and Costco, but there is no evidence to suggest that the products are manufactured in Canada.
Dual is a German-founded brand with its current headquarters in the United States. Its products are not manufactured in Canada.

Duramont is a company specializing in ergonomic office chairs. It was founded in May 2017 under the name 'The Brafman Group Inc.' and imports office chairs from Ningbo Nice Commodity Co. Ltd. in China. The company does not have a Canadian headquarters, and its products are not manufactured in Canada.

brand_origins.headquarters_location: St James Power Station, HarbourFront, Singapore
brand_origins.notes: Dyson is a British–Singaporean multinational headquartered in Singapore; its manufacturing operations are based in Singapore, Malaysia, and the Philippines, with no production in Canada.
Ecovacs was founded in 1998 and is headquartered in Suzhou, China, and it is not Canadian-owned or based; its products are manufactured in China, with no evidence of Canadian manufacturing.
Edifier is a Chinese audio equipment manufacturer founded in Beijing and now based in Shenzhen; it is not Canadian and does not manufacture its products in Canada.
ergoCentric is a Canadian ergonomic seating company headquartered in Mississauga, Ontario.
Eufy is a sub-brand of Anker Innovations, headquartered in Shenzhen, China; its products are designed and manufactured outside Canada.
Eureka is an American vacuum brand founded in Detroit in 1909 and headquartered in New Jersey; not Canadian-owned or manufactured in Canada.
Eurotech is an Italian company specializing in computer hardware, not office chairs.
Fellow is a US-based company founded in 2013, headquartered in San Francisco; its products are designed in the US and manufactured in China.

FiiO is a Chinese company specializing in audio equipment, including turntables. Their headquarters are in Guangzhou, China, and they are not a Canadian company. Their products are not made in Canada.
Fluance is a Canadian audio technology company headquartered in Niagara Falls, Ontario. It designs and manufactures high-fidelity turntables, powered speakers, and home audio loudspeakers.
Gates Corporation is headquartered in Denver, Colorado, USA, and does not manufacture office chairs in Canada.
Geek & Gorgeous is a Hungarian skincare brand headquartered in Budapest, Hungary, and its products are manufactured in Hungary.

Geologie is a U.S.-based skincare brand headquartered in New York City, specializing in personalized skincare routines. Their Vitamin C+E Ferulic Serum is formulated and manufactured in the United States.

brand_origins.headquarters_location: Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong, China
brand_origins.notes: Gevi is headquartered in Hong Kong with its official store there and its manufacturing based in Foshan, Guangdong Province, China; there is no evidence of Canadian ownership or production.
Global Furniture Group is a Canadian office furniture company headquartered in Toronto, Ontario.
Gourmia is headquartered in Brooklyn, NY, and its products are manufactured overseas (China, Hong Kong, Taiwan).
Founded and headquartered in Cambridge, Ontario; products are designed in Canada but primarily manufactured overseas.
Haden Appliances is a British kitchen-appliance brand headquartered in London; there is no indication it is Canadian or that its coffee makers are manufactured in Canada.
Hamilton Beach is an American company headquartered in Virginia; its products are manufactured primarily in China and Mexico.

Haworth is a privately held, family-owned office furniture manufacturer headquartered in Holland, Michigan, United States. Founded in 1948 by G.W. Haworth, the company designs and manufactures office furniture, including chairs, desks, and storage solutions. There is no evidence to suggest that Haworth is a Canadian company or that its products are made in Canada.
Herman Miller is an American company headquartered in Zeeland, Michigan, and is not a Canadian company. There is no evidence to suggest that their products are made or manufactured in Canada.
HON is an American company headquartered in Muscatine, Iowa, and its products are manufactured in the United States and China.

brand_origins.headquarters_location: Salt Lake City, Utah, United States
brand_origins.notes: House of Marley is an American company founded in 2010, headquartered in Salt Lake City, Utah, United States. It specializes in eco-friendly audio products, including turntables, and is not a Canadian brand.
Humanscale is headquartered in New York, USA, and has a showroom in Montreal, Canada. However, the company is not Canadian-owned, and its products are not manufactured in Canada.

Instant Brands, known for products like the Instant Pot, was founded in 2009 by Robert Wang, Yi Qin, and three other Canadian partners. Despite its Canadian founders, the company is headquartered in Downers Grove, Illinois, USA, and its products are manufactured outside of Canada.

IQAir is a Swiss air quality technology company founded in 1963 and headquartered in Steinach, Switzerland, with manufacturing based in Switzerland and Southern Germany; it has no Canadian ownership or manufacturing.
iRobot is an American company founded and headquartered in Bedford, Massachusetts, with no evidence of Canadian-based ownership or manufacturing.
Jan Marini Skin Research is headquartered in San Jose, California, USA, and is not a Canadian company. There is no evidence to suggest that their products are manufactured in Canada.
JBL is an American audio equipment manufacturer founded in 1946 and headquartered in Los Angeles; there is no evidence of Canadian ownership or product manufacturing in Canada.
Jerdon Style, LLC is headquartered in Richardson, Texas (United States) and its coffee makers are manufactured in China, with no indication of Canadian ownership or production.
JLab Audio is an American consumer audio brand founded in Tucson, Arizona in 2005 and headquartered in Carlsbad, California; its products are manufactured in China.

Kiehl's is an American skincare brand headquartered in New York City, USA. Their Powerful-Strength Vitamin C Serum is available in Canada, but the company is not Canadian-owned, and the products are not manufactured in Canada.
American home appliance brand owned by Whirlpool Corporation, headquartered in Benton Harbor, Michigan; some products are manufactured in Ontario and Quebec, Canada.
KORA Organics is an Australian-founded company headquartered in Warriewood, New South Wales, Australia. Its vitamin C serum is manufactured in Australia, not Canada.

Krups is a German kitchen appliance manufacturer headquartered in Solingen, Germany. It is part of the Groupe SEB and produces a variety of household appliances, including drip coffee makers. There is no evidence to suggest that Krups is a Canadian company or that its products are made in Canada.

La Roche-Posay is a French skincare brand founded in 1975 and is part of the L'Oréal Group. Its headquarters are in Paris, France, and it is not a Canadian company. The brand's products, including vitamin C serums, are not manufactured in Canada.
Levoit was founded in Anaheim, California in 2016 and remains headquartered in the United States; there is no indication of Canadian ownership or manufacturing.
Linn is a Scottish company founded in 1973, known for manufacturing the Sondek LP12 turntable. Their products are designed and assembled in Glasgow, Scotland.
Mad Hippie is an American skincare brand founded in Portland, Maine, and now headquartered in Austin, Texas. Their Vitamin C Serum is manufactured in the United States.
Majority is a British electronics company founded in 2012 in Cambridge, England. It designs and markets audio equipment, including turntables, but does not manufacture its products in Canada.

Mara is a California-based skincare brand founded by Allison McNamara, known for its algae-infused, marine-powered formulations. The Sea Vitamin C Serum is among their products, and the brand is sold in Canada through retailers like The Detox Market and Walmart Canada.
Marshall is a British company founded in London in 1962, headquartered in Milton Keynes, UK, with no evidence of Canadian ownership or manufacturing.

Medik8 is a British skincare brand founded in 2009, known for its vitamin C serums. The company is headquartered in London, England, and manufactures its products in the UK. In June 2025, L'Oréal acquired a majority stake in Medik8, integrating it into their L'Oréal Luxe division. Therefore, Medik8 is not a Canadian vitamin C serum brand.
Melitta is a German company founded in 1908 and headquartered in Minden, Germany; its drip coffee makers and coffee filters are manufactured in the US and overseas, with no Canadian manufacturing.
Mila is headquartered in San Francisco (founded 2015) with no indication of Canadian incorporation or manufacturing in Canada.

Monolith by Monoprice is a brand of turntables produced by Monoprice, an American company headquartered in Brea, California. The Monolith turntables are manufactured by Audio-Technica, a Japanese company, indicating that the products are not made in Canada.
MOVA is a global sub-brand of Spacewalker Technology Hong Kong Co., Ltd., headquartered in Hong Kong; its products are manufactured in China.
Mr. Coffee is an American brand founded and headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio, owned by Newell Brands, and its drip coffee makers are manufactured in China, not Canada.
Mueller Home is headquartered in Pomona, California, and their drip coffee makers are manufactured in China, not Canada.
Music Hall is an American company founded in 1985, specializing in high-quality audio components, including turntables. Their products are manufactured in the Czech Republic, not Canada.
Narwal Robotics is a Chinese technology company founded in 2016 by Zhang Junbin, headquartered in Shenzhen, China, with its manufacturing operations based in China rather than Canada.

Naturium is a U.S.-based skincare brand founded by Susan Yara in 2019. The brand's products, including the Vitamin C Complex Serum, are not manufactured in Canada. While Naturium has expanded its availability to Canadian consumers through platforms like Amazon Canada, it remains a U.S.-based company with products made elsewhere.
Nightingale is a Canadian office seating company headquartered in Mississauga, Ontario.
Ninja is a subsidiary of SharkNinja, headquartered in Needham, Massachusetts; founded in Montreal but now a U.S. company; products are manufactured in China.

Nouhaus is a U.S.-based company specializing in ergonomic office chairs and other furniture. Their headquarters are located in Los Angeles, California, and their products are not manufactured in Canada.

NuWave LLC is a U.S.-based company founded in the early 1990s and headquartered in Vernon Hills, IL; its air purifiers are designed and sold primarily in the U.S., with no indication of Canadian ownership or manufacturing.
Oak Hollow Furniture is a U.S.-based company specializing in ergonomic office chairs, founded in early 2020. Their products are not manufactured in Canada.
Obagi Medical is headquartered in Irvine, California, USA, and its products are not manufactured in Canada.
Office Star Products is headquartered in Ontario, California, USA, and operates a warehouse in Concord, Ontario, Canada, indicating that some of their products are made or assembled in Canada.

brand_origins.headquarters_location: New York City, New York, United States
brand_origins.notes: OXO is an American company founded in 1990 and headquartered in New York City; its products are manufactured in the USA and China.

Paderno (operated by Padinox Inc.) was founded in 1979 and is headquartered in Winsloe, PEI, Canada; while it manufactures cookware there, its drip coffee makers are designed in Canada but not clearly produced in its Canadian plant.

Paula’s Choice is a U.S.-based skincare company founded in 1995 and headquartered in Seattle, Washington. The company was acquired by Unilever in June 2021. Their products, including vitamin C serums, are manufactured in the United States.
Peter Thomas Roth is a skincare brand headquartered in New York, NY, United States. Their products, including the Potent-C Power Serum, are not manufactured in Canada.

Pioneer DJ is a Japanese company specializing in DJ equipment, including turntables. It was established as an independent entity in 2014, with its headquarters located in Yokohama, Japan. The company is not Canadian-owned, and there is no evidence to suggest that its products are manufactured in Canada.
Pro-Ject is an Austrian company founded in 1991, with its headquarters in Mistelbach, Lower Austria, Austria. Its products are manufactured in Germany, the Czech Republic, and Slovakia.

PSB Speakers is a Canadian‐founded and headquartered audio company (est. 1972), now part of Lenbrook Group; although they design and sell noise-cancelling over-ear headphones (M4U series), there is no indication their products are manufactured in Canada.
Puro Sound Labs was founded and is headquartered in San Diego, California, with no indication of Canadian ownership or manufacturing.
Rabbit Air is a U.S.-founded and headquartered air purifier company based in Downey, California, with no indication of Canadian ownership or manufacturing.
Ratio is headquartered in Portland, Oregon, USA, was founded in 2012, and its drip coffee makers (e.g., the Four) are manufactured abroad (e.g., in China).

Rega Research Ltd. is a British audio equipment manufacturer founded in 1973, known for its turntables, cartridges, and tonearms. The company is headquartered in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, England, and designs and assembles its products in the UK. Rega's products are not made in Canada.

Rekkord is a German turntable manufacturer headquartered in St. Georgen, Baden-Württemberg, Germany. Their turntables are handcrafted in their own factory in the Black Forest region, with 99% of components sourced from local suppliers. The company has a legacy of nearly a century in precision engineering and clock-making, with their original factory opening in 1907. Rekkord specializes in automatic turntables, with their products being distributed in North America by VANA Ltd. and in Europe by AV EMEA.
Revision Skincare is a U.S.-based company headquartered in Irving, Texas, and its products are manufactured in the United States.
Roborock is headquartered and founded in China; there is no evidence of Canadian ownership or manufacturing.
Incorporated and headquartered in Canada since 1985; products are sourced and assembled overseas with no indication of manufacturing in Canada.

Secretlab is a Singaporean company founded in 2014, specializing in gaming chairs. Its headquarters are located in Singapore, and it operates a factory in China. There is no evidence to suggest that Secretlab is a Canadian company or that its products are made in Canada.

Sennheiser is a German audio company founded in 1945 and headquartered in Wedemark, Germany, with manufacturing sites in Germany, Ireland, the United States, Romania, and China, but no facilities in Canada.

SENTÉ is a U.S.-based skincare company specializing in medical-grade products, including vitamin C serums. Their headquarters are located in San Diego, California, and their products are manufactured in the United States.

Shardor is a Chinese company established in 2018, specializing in manufacturing electric coffee grinders, milk frothers, drip coffee makers, professional espresso machines, and French presses. Their products are sold in Canada, but the company is not Canadian-owned or headquartered in Canada.
Shark is part of SharkNinja, an American company headquartered in Needham, Massachusetts; its products are manufactured overseas, not in Canada.
Sharp Corporation is a Japanese electronics company founded in 1912 and headquartered in Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. There is no indication that its air purifiers are manufactured in Canada.
Sidiz is a South Korean company specializing in ergonomic office chairs, with manufacturing facilities in South Korea and Vietnam.

Sihoo is a Chinese company specializing in ergonomic office chairs, with its manufacturing facilities located in Foshan, Guangdong, China. The company has a presence in over 100 countries, including Canada, where its products are available through retailers like Walmart Canada. However, Sihoo is not a Canadian-founded or Canadian-owned company, and its products are not made or manufactured in Canada.
SkinCeuticals is an American brand headquartered in New York, NY, USA, and is not a Canadian company. Their products are not manufactured in Canada.
Smart Air is headquartered in Beijing, China and there is no indication its products are made or assembled in Canada.

Sony is a Japanese multinational founded and headquartered in Tokyo; its over-ear noise-cancelling headphones are produced in facilities in Japan (e.g., Ōita Prefecture) and other countries, but not in Canada.
Soundpeats is headquartered in Shenzhen, China and there is no indication it is Canadian or that its products are made in Canada.

Staples is an American multinational office supply retail company headquartered in Framingham, Massachusetts. While it operates in Canada through Staples Canada, a subsidiary, the parent company is not Canadian-founded, Canadian-owned, or headquartered in Canada. Additionally, there is no clear evidence that Staples products, including office chairs, are manufactured in Canada.
Steelcase is an American company headquartered in Grand Rapids, Michigan, and its products are not manufactured in Canada.
Summer Fridays is a U.S.-based skincare brand founded in Los Angeles, California. Their products, including the CC Me Vitamin C Serum, are not manufactured in Canada.
Sunday Riley is a U.S.-based skincare brand headquartered in Houston, Texas. Their products, including the C.E.O. 15% Vitamin C Brightening Serum, are sold in Canada but are not manufactured there.

SYBO Kitchen is a U.S.-based company (1484 Old Middlefield Way, Mountain View, CA 94043) and explicitly markets itself as a proud American brand, with no indication that its products are manufactured in Canada.
TechDAS is a Japanese high-end turntable brand under Stella Inc., with all products made in Japan.

Technics is a Japanese audio brand established by Matsushita Electric (now Panasonic) in 1965. Its headquarters are located in Kadoma, Osaka, Japan. Technics products, including turntables, are manufactured in Japan and are not made in Canada.
Technivorm is a family-owned Dutch manufacturer headquartered in Amerongen, Netherlands; all Moccamaster machines are handmade in Amerongen.
Teknion is a Canadian office furniture company headquartered in Toronto, Ontario.

Tempur-Pedic, a subsidiary of Tempur Sealy International, is headquartered in Lexington, Kentucky, USA. While Tempur-Pedic Canada operates in London, Ontario, it is a subsidiary of the U.S. parent company, and their office chairs are manufactured in Taiwan.
The Inkey List is a UK-based skincare brand founded in 2018 by Colette Laxton and Mark Curry. Their products, including the Vitamin C serum, are not manufactured in Canada.

The Ordinary is a Canadian-founded skincare brand headquartered in Toronto, Ontario, Canada. However, there is no clear evidence that their products, including vitamin C serums, are manufactured in Canada.
Thinksound was founded in 2009 and is headquartered in Toronto, Ontario; no clear evidence its over-ear headphones are manufactured in Canada.
Timeless Skin Care is a U.S.-based company specializing in vitamin C serums and other skincare products. Their official website does not indicate Canadian ownership or manufacturing.

U Beauty is an American skincare brand founded in 2019 by Tina Chen Craig and Katie Borghese. The company is headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida, United States, and its products are not manufactured in Canada.
U-Turn Audio is an American company headquartered in Woburn, Massachusetts, and its products are manufactured in the United States.

Vertere Acoustics is a British high-end audio manufacturer founded in 2006 by Touraj Moghaddam, a former co-founder of Roksan. The company is headquartered in London, England, and is dedicated to crafting exceptional audio products. Vertere's products are distributed in North America by Rutherford Audio Inc., based in Ferndale, Washington, USA. There is no evidence to suggest that Vertere is a Canadian company or that its products are made in Canada.

Via Seating is a U.S.-based company founded in 1987, specializing in office seating solutions. Their headquarters are located in Sparks, Nevada, USA. There is no evidence to suggest that Via Seating is a Canadian company or that their products are made in Canada.
Victrola is an American brand headquartered in New York, New York, USA. Their products are not manufactured in Canada.

VPI Industries is an American company founded in 1978 by Harry and Sheila Weisfeld in Cliffwood, New Jersey. They are known for manufacturing high-end turntables and audio equipment, all produced in the USA.

Wilson Benesch is a British company founded in 1989, specializing in high-end audio equipment, including turntables. Their headquarters are located in Sheffield, South Yorkshire, England, and they manufacture their products in-house in the UK.
Wyze is an American company based in Kirkland, Washington; its products are manufactured in China.

Yeedi was launched in Shenzhen in 2019 and its product design, R&D and manufacturing are based in Shenzhen, China. There is no indication of Canadian founding, ownership, headquarters or manufacturing.

Yon-Ka Paris is a French skincare brand headquartered in Paris, France. Their products, including the Vitamin C serum, are formulated, manufactured, tested, and distributed by Laboratoires Multaler, located outside of Paris. Therefore, Yon-Ka Paris is not a Canadian vitamin C serum brand.
Yonisee is a brand offering office chairs, but there is no available information about its country of origin or manufacturing location.
